content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

March 3, 1986

Pamela Townsend  
Modoc County Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO)  
202 West 4th Street  
Alturas, CA 96101

Re: Your Request for Advice  
Our Advice No. A-86-056

Dear Ms. Townsend:

I have been asked to reply to your letter dated February 10, 1986. In that letter you ask if appointed public members, the executive officer, planning director or planners of a LAFCO must file statements of economic interests.

Before any of these officials incur any reporting obligations, the LAFCO must first adopt a conflict of interest code.

A conflict of interest code is a document which lists the positions within the agency that are in decisionmaking capacities and further provides disclosure categories which describe the types of information to be reported by the employees designated in the code. The categories are designed to require disclosure of the financial interests which the designated employee can affect by decisions he or she makes in his or her official capacity.

You would not designate in the code your county supervisors who are members of your LAFCO (they are already covered under Government Code Section 87200 and have county-wide jurisdiction), nor would you designate other county employees who are required to file under the County's conflict of interest code and have full disclosure.

You will need to develop a code to cover any other positions within your agency, such as the executive officer and the appointed public members.

Pamela Townsend  
March 3, 1986  
Page 2

While I do not have a sample code of other LAFCOs for you to follow, I do have a package of materials available which should be of assistance to you in developing your code. The code, once adopted by the LAFCO, is to be submitted to your Board of Supervisors for approval.

If you have any further questions or need assistance in the development of your code, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Jeanette E. Turvill  
Legal Assistant  
Legal Division

JET:plh  
Enclosures

February 10, 1986

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Adviser:

Please reply in regard to the following:

(1) Must the appointed public members of LAFCO file statement of economic interest?

(2) Must the appointed Executive Officer of LAFCO, Planning Director or Planners file a statement of economic interest?

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Pamela Townsend
Assistant Executive Officer
